legal_notice.title: Clinical Record Seal
content: Do not include patient names, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, addresses,
or any other protected health information (PHI) in this submission.
Seal the institutional record — not patient data. Describe events by type, context, and significance only.
This seal proves the record existed in this exact form before any review, remediation, or external pressure.

fields.label: Submitter Name or Role
fields.description: Your name or role. Include institution name if submitting on behalf of an organization.

fields.label: Institution or Organization
fields.description: The institution this record belongs to.

fields.label: Record Type
fields.description: What kind of record is being sealed?
fields.placeholder: Incident Report / Trial Data / Protocol Version / Adverse Event / Audit Finding

fields.label: Date of Record Creation
fields.description: The date this record was created or the event occurred. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

fields.label: Record Description
fields.description: Describe the record — no PHI. Use de-identified descriptions: event type, department, context, significance.
fields.placeholder: Describe the record without any protected health information.

fields.label: Status at Time of Sealing
fields.description: What is the status right now? Before review, before remediation, before audit.

fields.label: Regulatory Framework (Optional)
fields.description: What regulatory framework applies to this record?
fields.placeholder: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 / IRB Protocol / CMS / HIPAA / Other

fields.label: Supporting Files (Optional)
fields.description: Upload de-identified supporting documents only. No PHI permitted in uploaded files.

fields.label: Declaration — Required
options: I affirm this submission contains no protected health information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA.
I affirm this record accurately represents the institutional state at the time of sealing.
I am authorized to submit this record on behalf of my institution.
I understand this submission is immutable — it cannot be altered after sealing.
I understand the protocol provides a tamper-evident timestamp — not legal or regulatory advice.

steps: PHI check — Reviewed for any inadvertent PHI before sealing.
Sealing — Sealed with a FROZEN-2.0 triple-time cryptographic stamp. Permanent.
Ledger entry — Enters the Sovereign Trace ledger with institution name, timestamp, and SHA-256 hash.
Notification — You receive your ledger entry ID and SHA-256 hash.
after_submit.footer:
The seal is permanent. The seal is the pre-review record.

Sovereign Trace Protocol | Sheldon K. Salmon | aionsystem2026@gmail.com
